data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`), but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't know about it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `@(Compile)` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target generates a `.cs` file into the intermediate output path, but it doesn't add the file to the `Compile` item group. The target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"`, which means the file is written before compilation, but MSBuild's `Compile` items are already resolved by then. The generated file needs to be added to the `@(Compile)` item group so the compiler picks it up.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item inside the target after generating the file.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>"}

<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `@(Compile)` item group. MSBuild's compile item list was already finalized, so the compiler never saw the generated file.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` after the file is written, so the compiler picks it up during `CoreCompile`.
